raw_91_c3: FIRST TICK = PASS 1 corpus-class auto-detection (file_size + fingerprint + schema header + rule_density H_n approx) + PASS 2 per-class algorithm dispatch table (small-file→A23 v2, text/JSON/struct→A18 v2, synthetic-repetitive→A24, passthrough <64B/already-encoded) + PASS 3 encode wrapper minimum (header + base64-of-inner + AOT subprocess delegation) + PASS 4 decode wrapper minimum (header parse + base64 + inner-decode delegation) + 5-fixture selftest classification accuracy + idempotent passthrough. raw 91 honest C3: A25 = META-DISPATCHER (corpus-class detection + per-class winner routing), NOT a new compression algorithm. The +pp aggregate gain is from selecting the right inner algorithm per class, NOT from any novel compression — inner algorithms (A18 v2 / A23 v2 / A24) are unchanged. CRITICAL HONEST FINDING: on the 4-class slice (n6 atlas + aot_cache_gc + audit + 95-file nexus, total 209,425 bytes), A25 dispatcher delivered +0.073pp aggregate lift over A18-only baseline (133,495 saved vs 133,343 saved). Per-file on small-file class where A23 strictly beats A18: avg +5pp delta (n=11/95 winning files). Projected +3..+5pp aggregate gain DID NOT materialize on 4-class measured slice because (1) small-file class is byte-light vs large classes, (2) most small files <500B passthrough as incompressible, (3) A18 v2 already wins on >80% of bytes. 80% target NOT reached on this slice; gap remains 4.0pp. raw 137 cmix-ban MAINTAINED: A25 = deterministic class-detection (integer counters + threshold table) + dispatch table (class→algo string lookup) + delegation to deterministic A18/A23/A24. NO fp ops, NO neural mixer.
